title: Three-tiered Children’s Shelf
summary: This cute kids shelf has clean lines and a mid-century vibe!
description: This cute kids shelf has clean lines and a mid-century vibe! Perfect for toys, books, stuffed animals, etc! It doesn’t HAVE to be for kid stuff though. This shelf would be great for your craft room, pantry, laundry room, & more! More details instructions on our website here: https://bit.ly/2LUSFgZ

materials.name: #8 1-1/4 in. Square Maxi-Loc Head Coarse Zinc-Plated Steel Pocket-Hole Screw (100-Pack)
#8 2 in. T-10 FIN/Trim Trim Head Screw GRK Fasteners
#8 1 3/8 in. T-10 FIN/Trim Trim Head Screw GRK Fasteners
3/8 dowel (Oak) or Kreg Pocket hole plugs

steps.title: Cut Plywood
steps.body: I broke my sheet stock down to a more manageable size with a circular saw, then cut the remaining parts on the table saw.

steps.title: Cut Dimension Lumber
steps.body: Using a table saw, rip the 5/4 x 6 x 96" into 2" strips.

steps.title: Cutting the Leg components
steps.body: Using a Kreg Precision miter gauge, cut the components of the leg.

steps.title: Cutting the Shelf Sides/Dividers
steps.body: Using a table saw crosscut sled (80 degrees), cut the 10 degree angles. Tip: Mark the location of the angles by dry fitting the shelves and scribing the dividers and sides where the top of the front meets them.

steps.title: Drill Pocket holes
steps.body:
Drill pocket holes.
(2 holes) front/inside of Shelf Sides (2 holes) both ends of the Shelf Backs (3 holes) sides of the Shelf Bottoms (4 holes) front of the Shelf Bottoms (2 holes) both ends of the Leg Tops

steps.title: Shelves Assembly
steps.body:
Attach the Shelf Sides to the Shelf Fronts, then attach the back to the sides, for the shelves with dividers attach them to the Shelf Front with 18 ga brad nails, then screw the Shelf Back to the Shelf Dividers with #8 FIN/Trim screws.
Screw the Shelf Bottoms to the Shelf Front and Shelf Sides with 1 1/4 pocket screws and lastly, screw the the bottom to the Shelf Back with #8 FIN/Trim screws.
TIP: Predrill the holes for the #8 FIN/Trim screws with a 5/64 drill bit to keep the plywood from splitting out.

steps.title: Prep for Finish
steps.body:
Layout the shelf locations on the legs, then round the edges of the legs using a 1/4" Round-over Router bit. Routee the entire outside edge of the legs. On the inside of the legs route the areas around the shelves.
Fill the top two holes on the shelf legs with Dowels or with Kreg pocket hole plugs.

steps.title: Final Assembly
steps.body: Layout the shelf locations on the inside of the legs screw the shelves to the legs using #8 FIN/Trim screws 1 3/8".
